mnemonics: PANDA: Pandas is like a powerful panda that manipulates data. Remember: Pandas = DataFrames + Series. Use iloc for integer position, loc for label indexing.
keyPoints: DataFrames are 2D labeled data structures with columns of potentially different types
Series are 1D labeled arrays capable of holding any data type
Use loc for label-based indexing and iloc for integer position-based indexing
Boolean indexing allows filtering data based on conditions
Handling missing data is crucial with methods like isnull(), dropna(), fillna()
commonMistakes: Confusing loc (label-based) and iloc (integer position-based) indexing
Forgetting to use double brackets df[['column']] when selecting multiple columns
Not handling missing values before analysis leading to errors
Modifying a slice of DataFrame without using .copy() leading to SettingWithCopyWarning
Using chained indexing (e.g., df[df['age'] > 30]['name']) which can cause issues
